channel.title: Seagate Technology — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Seagate Technology (seagate).

item.title: CVE-2026-10719 · 1.8 LOW — Open Seachest/Seachest NVMe show Format Descriptors Vulnerability
item.description: Out of bounds write in openSeaChest’s --showSupportedFormats in Seagate’s openSeaChest v25.05.3 on all supported platforms allows for writing 1 extra byte outside of allocated memory which sets a value to 1 via a maliciously crafted NVMe device with…

item.title: CVE-2026-10718 · 4.6 MEDIUM — Open Seachest/Seachest NVMe Trim (Deallocate) Vulnerability
item.description: Out of bounds write in openSeaChest’s Trim/Unmap operation in Seagate’s openSeaChest v26.03.0 on all supported platforms allows for writing extra memory describing a range of LBAs to deallocate 16 bytes outside of the allocated space when running thi…

item.title: CVE-2026-10717 · 1.8 LOW — Open-Seachest/Seachest show SCSI Defect List Vulnerability
item.description: Out of bounds write and reads in openSeaChest’s --showSCSIDefects in Seagate’s openSeaChest v25.05.3 on all supported platforms allows for writing defect information out of bounds for very large defects lists via a very bad drive with lots of defects…

item.title: CVE-2025-9267 · 7 HIGH
item.description: In Seagate Toolkit on Windows a vulnerability exists in the Toolkit Installer prior to versions 2.35.0.6 where it attempts to load DLLs from the current working directory without validating their origin or integrity. This behavior can be exploited by…

item.title: CVE-2025-9043 · 6.7 MEDIUM
item.description: The service executable path in Seagate Toolkit on Versions prior to 2.34.0.33 on Windows allows an attacker with Admin privileges to exploit a vulnerability as classified under CWE-428: Unquoted Search Path or Element. An attacker with write permissi…
